keywords: payment terms, Letter of Credit, T/T, D/P, international trade, fastener import payment

body.en: Common payment methods for fastener/a> imports from China: 1. T/T (Telegraphic Transfer): - Buyer transfers money directly to seller's bank - Most common for established relationships - Terms: 30% deposit, 70% before shipment - Pros: Simple, low cost - Cons: No payment protection 2. L/C (Letter of Credit): - Bank guarantees payment upon presentation of documents - Pros: Maximum protection for both parties - Cons: Expensive, complex 3. D/P (Documents Against Payment): - Documents released upon payment at destination - Pros: Some protection for seller - Cons: Buyer must pay before receiving goods 4. D/A (Documents Against Acceptance): - Documents released against acceptance of draft - Pros: Allows buyer time to sell goods before paying - Cons: Higher risk for seller Related resources: high-tensile bolts anchor bolts stainless steel fasteners hex bolts lock nuts washers self-drilling screws socket screws. This procurement framework applies to fastener sourcing for construction, mining, energy, automotive, and general industrial applications. The decision logic prioritizes quality consistency, supplier reliability, and total cost of ownership over short-term unit price. For B2B buyers in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia, additional considerations include regional customs requirements, container consolidation options, and payment security mechanisms. Standardized specifications that travel across projects reduce training time, RFQ processing time, and supplier onboarding effort. Payment terms recommendations by relationship level:

New Suppliers:
- 100% T/T before shipment
- Or 30% deposit, 70% against copy of B/L

Established Relationship (6+ months):
- 30% deposit, 70% T/T against B/L copy
- Or 30% deposit, 70% D/P at sight

Long-term Partners (1+ year):
- 30% deposit, 70% T/T 7-15 days after shipment
- Consider open account terms for trusted suppliers

Large Orders ($50,000+):
- 20% deposit, 80% in two installments
- Consider Letter of Credit for protection  Standards-based procurement requires continuous learning as ISO, ASTM, DIN, and GB standards are revised every 3-5 years. Subscribe to standards update notifications and review specifications annually. The most common standards used in international fastener trade are ISO 898-1 (mechanical properties), ISO 3506-1 (stainless), ISO 3269 (acceptance inspection), ISO 4042 (coatings), and ISO 1502 (thread gauges). For B2B buyers, building a standards reference library with at least the top 20 standards relevant to your product mix is essential. Protecting yourself in international fastener/a> trade: 1. Verify Supplier: - Business license verification - Factory audit before first order - Reference checks from other buyers 2. Use Secure Payment Channels: - Use bank-to-bank transfers - Avoid Western Union or similar - Keep payment records 3. Document Everything: - Clear contract with specifications - Inspections before payment - Quality agreements in writing 4. Start Small: - Test order before large commitment - Build trust gradually - Increase terms as relationship develops The comparison methodology above uses typical ranges from industry data and TradeGo procurement experience 2018-2024. Actual market prices fluctuate with raw material costs (steel, zinc, nickel), energy costs, labor costs, and currency exchange rates. For accurate comparison, request identical RFQs from 3-5 qualified suppliers and compare landed cost (product + shipping + insurance + duties). q.en: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.zh: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.es: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.ar: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.fr: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.pt: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.ru: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.ja: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.de: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?
q.hi: What payment terms are safest for importing fasteners from China to Africa?

a.en: For Africa importers, the safest sequence is: T/T 30% deposit to start production, then T/T 70% against copy of Bill of Lading. This gives the buyer leverage — the supplier only gets full payment when goods are shipped. For orders over $50,000, consider Letters of Credit (L/C) through a reputable African bank. Avoid Western Union or other irreversible payment methods for large orders.

q.en: What are the costs associated with Letters of Credit for fastener imports?
q.zh: What are the costs associated with Letters of Credit for fastener imports?

q.ar: What are the costs associated with Letters of Credit for fastener imports?

q.de: What are the costs associated with Letters of Credit for fastener imports?
q.hi: What are the costs associated with Letters of Credit for fastener imports?

a.en: L/C costs typically include: advising fee (0.1% of L/C value, min $150-300), confirmation fee (0.2-0.5% for African banks), and negotiation fees. Total L/C costs for a $100,000 fastener order typically run $800-2,000. Despite the cost, L/Cs provide strong payment security and are recommended for new supplier relationships or orders over $30,000.

q.en: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.zh: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.es: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.ar: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.fr: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.pt: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.ru: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.ja: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.de: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?
q.hi: How can I verify a Chinese fastener supplier's legitimacy before paying?

a.en: Verify by: (1) Request company business license and export license; (2) Use SGS or CCIC to inspect goods before shipment; (3) Check their registration on Alibaba/Made-in-China and review transaction history; (4) Conduct video factory tour; (5) Check Chinese business registry (tianyancha.com); (6) Request references from past African buyers. Never pay 100% advance to a new supplier.

The verification checklist applies to both initial supplier qualification and ongoing batch inspection. For high-risk applications (load-bearing structural, pressure vessels, mining, marine), add fatigue testing, salt-spray testing (ASTM B117), torque-tension testing, and post-installation inspection. The cost of additional verification is 0.1-0.3% of order value, while undetected quality failures can be 10-100x the order value in damages.
